edges.description: Loss of LAL activity leads to lysosomal neutral-lipid accumulation.
edges.target: Lysosomal Cholesteryl Ester and Triglyceride Accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Lysosomal Acid Lipase Deficiency: A Report of Two Cases and a Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: a homozygous mutation of the LIPA gene was identified

nodes.description: Biallelic LIPA variants with residual activity reduce lysosomal acid lipase, the sole enzyme that hydrolyzes neutral lipids (cholesteryl esters and triglycerides) within the lysosome.

evidence.reference_title: Recent insights into lysosomal acid lipase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Lysosomal acid lipase (LAL) is the sole enzyme known to degrade neutral lipids in the lysosome. Mutations in the LAL-encoding LIPA gene lead to rare lysosomal lipid storage disorders with complete or partial absence of LAL activity

nodes.description: Undegraded cholesteryl esters and triglycerides accumulate in lysosomes of hepatocytes and macrophages, primarily in the liver and spleen, driving hepatic and dyslipidemic disease.
nodes.id: Lysosomal Cholesteryl Ester and Triglyceride Accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Lysosomal Acid Lipase Deficiency: A Report of Two Cases and a Review of the Literature.
evidence.snippet: characterised by the formation of cholesterol esters and triglyceride storages, primarily in the liver and spleen
